criteria.text: Log360 server is running with a dedicated service account (not LocalSystem or Administrator)
criteria.recommendation: Configure Log360 to run under a dedicated domain service account with minimum permissions. The service account needs: Log on as a service right, read access to integrated component APIs, and read access to SIEM data sources. Avoid Domain Admin membership.

criteria.text: Log360 is on the latest available build (13000 series)
criteria.recommendation: Update to the latest 13000-series build immediately. This series introduces major capabilities: AI Alert Investigation Agent, Zia AI Assistant, native SOAR, re-architected detection engine with 1,500+ MITRE ATT&CK rules, native UEBA integration, and Access Gateway Clusters. Check Admin > Product Updates.

criteria.text: Log360 server meets hardware requirements for integrated suite deployment
criteria.recommendation: Log360 integrates multiple components (EventLog Analyzer, ADAudit Plus, DataSecurity Plus) on the same platform. Ensure dedicated hardware: minimum 32GB RAM, 12 CPU cores, 1TB SSD for production. Log360 is the central nervous system of your security monitoring—inadequate resources cause event drops and alerting failures.

criteria.text: External database (MSSQL/PostgreSQL) is configured for production
criteria.recommendation: Use an external MSSQL or PostgreSQL database for all production Log360 deployments. External databases support HA, enterprise backup, and better query performance. Bundled databases are not suitable for enterprise-scale deployments. Configure under Admin > Database.

criteria.text: High Availability (HA) is configured for the Log360 platform
criteria.recommendation: Configure Log360 HA to prevent single points of failure in your primary SIEM. HA failover should be tested quarterly. For distributed deployments, consider Active/Passive HA with automatic failover. A failed SIEM during an active incident is a critical operational failure.

criteria.text: Access Gateway Clusters are configured for secure remote access to Log360 (March 2026 feature)
criteria.recommendation: Implement Access Gateway Clusters (introduced March 2026) for secure distributed/hybrid architecture access. This enables secure UI access without direct VPN to the Log360 server, supports zero-trust network access principles, and reduces attack surface for the SIEM console.

criteria.text: Log360 license includes all required component modules (ELA, ADAudit, DataSecurity Plus)
criteria.recommendation: Verify all required component licenses are active: EventLog Analyzer (log count), ADAudit Plus (DC count), DataSecurity Plus (file server count). Expired component licenses silently stop collection for that data source. Navigate to Admin > License to verify.

criteria.text: SOAR module is licensed and enabled
criteria.recommendation: Ensure the native SOAR module license is active to enable incident response automation. SOAR is one of the most impactful capabilities in the 13000 series—it can dramatically reduce MTTR. Navigate to Admin > License > SOAR Module to verify.

criteria.text: UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics) is enabled as native capability
criteria.recommendation: Verify UEBA is enabled as a native feature in the 13000-series build. Note: UEBA is now natively integrated—there is no separate Log360 UEBA product. Navigate to Log360 > UEBA > Dashboard to confirm it's active and the anomaly models are running.

criteria.text: AI Alert Investigation Agent is enabled and configured
criteria.recommendation: Enable the AI Alert Investigation Agent (new in 13000 series). This LLM-powered agent autonomously: extracts affected entities, maps alerts to MITRE ATT&CK tactics, generates attack timelines, suggests remediation steps, auto-closes false positives, and flags duplicate alerts. Navigate to AI > Alert Investigation Agent > Configure.

criteria.text: LLM provider is configured for AI Investigation Agent (OpenAI or Azure OpenAI)
criteria.recommendation: Configure the LLM backend for the AI Investigation Agent: choose between OpenAI (GPT-4) or Azure OpenAI (for compliance-sensitive environments where data must stay within your Azure tenant). Provide your API key via BYO (Bring Your Own) Key model. Configure under AI > LLM Settings > Provider Configuration.

criteria.text: Auto-closure and duplicate flagging is configured for AI agent
criteria.recommendation: Configure the AI agent's auto-closure and deduplication settings. Start conservatively: enable auto-closure only for alerts with >95% confidence of being false positive, and require analyst review for medium-confidence auto-closures. This reduces alert backlog while maintaining human oversight.

criteria.text: Zia AI Assistant (natural language query) is configured and analyst team is trained
criteria.recommendation: Enable Zia AI Assistant to allow analysts to query logs and incidents using natural language ('Show all failed logons from external IPs in the last 24 hours'). Train analysts to use Zia for investigation acceleration. Configure under AI > Zia Settings. Zia can reduce investigation time by 60-80%.

criteria.text: MITRE ATT&CK attack timeline generation is enabled in AI Agent
criteria.recommendation: Enable attack timeline generation in the AI Investigation Agent. For each security incident, the AI agent automatically generates a chronological timeline of events mapped to MITRE ATT&CK stages. This dramatically improves incident response quality and reduces analyst cognitive load during crisis situations.

criteria.text: SOAR playbooks are created for critical incident types (phishing, account compromise, ransomware)
criteria.recommendation: Create SOAR playbooks for your top 3-5 incident types. Use the low-code playbook builder in Log360's native SOAR. Prioritize: Account Compromise Response, Ransomware Containment, Phishing Response, DDoS Mitigation, Insider Threat Response. Use pre-built templates as starting points.

criteria.text: Pre-built incident response templates are customized and deployed
criteria.recommendation: Review and customize all pre-built SOAR incident response templates provided in the template library. Customize: notification recipients, escalation thresholds, approved remediation actions, and approval workflows for destructive actions. Document the business justification for each automated action.

criteria.text: Automated response actions are configured and tested (endpoint isolation, session revocation, artifact enrichment)
criteria.recommendation: Configure and test automated response actions in SOAR: endpoint isolation via EDR API (CrowdStrike, BitDefender), Active Directory account disable, Kerberos ticket revocation, IP blocking via firewall API, and VirusTotal hash lookup for artifact enrichment. Test each action in a staging environment before production deployment.

criteria.text: Python/Deluge SOAR scripts are version-controlled and peer-reviewed
criteria.recommendation: Store all SOAR playbook code (Python/Deluge scripts) in version control (Git). Require peer review for all production playbook changes. Malicious or buggy SOAR code can cause production outages or unintended security actions. Maintain staging and production playbook environments.

criteria.text: Zoho Qntrl integration is configured for advanced workflow orchestration if applicable
criteria.recommendation: Configure Zoho Qntrl integration if you use Zoho's business process automation platform. This enables no-code workflow creation, approval workflows, SLA tracking, and integration with 500+ business applications through Qntrl's connector library.

criteria.text: SOAR playbook execution logs are reviewed and MTTR improvement is tracked
criteria.recommendation: Regularly review SOAR execution logs to: track automated actions taken, identify playbook failures, measure MTTR improvement vs. manual response. Target: 80% reduction in MTTR for automated incident types. Navigate to SOAR > Execution History to review performance metrics.

criteria.text: Re-architected detection engine (13000 series) is active with central detection console
criteria.recommendation: Verify the new detection engine in the 13000 series is fully operational. The re-architected engine provides: central detection console, object-level filters, cloud-delivered rule updates, and 1,500+ MITRE ATT&CK-aligned detection rules. Navigate to Detection > Detection Console to verify status.

criteria.text: 1,500+ cloud-delivered MITRE ATT&CK-aligned detection rules are enabled and up-to-date
criteria.recommendation: Ensure all 1,500+ cloud-delivered detection rules are enabled and synced with the latest updates. These rules cover all MITRE ATT&CK tactics and techniques with real-world threat actor TTPs. Enable automatic rule updates to stay current with emerging threats. Navigate to Detection > Rules > Update Rules.

criteria.text: Object-level filters are configured in the detection engine for noise reduction
criteria.recommendation: Configure object-level filters to exclude known-good entities from detection rules: service account authentication, scheduled backup tasks, monitoring agent activities. Well-tuned object-level filters reduce false positive rate without weakening detection coverage. Navigate to Detection > Detection Rules > Filter Configuration.

criteria.text: Custom detection rules are created for organization-specific threat models
criteria.recommendation: Supplement cloud-delivered rules with custom detection rules based on your specific threat model: insider threat scenarios, supply chain attack patterns, environment-specific attack paths. Use the rule builder to create rules without writing code. Document each custom rule's threat hypothesis.

criteria.text: UEBA anomaly models are active and baseline learning period has completed (30+ days)
criteria.recommendation: Allow UEBA at least 30 days of baseline data collection before enabling anomaly alerts. Premature alert activation causes excessive false positives. Monitor the baseline completeness indicator in UEBA > Model Status. The 13000-series UEBA is natively integrated into the alert triage workflow.

criteria.text: User risk scoring is configured and high-risk users are reviewed daily
criteria.recommendation: Configure user risk scoring thresholds and establish a daily review process for users with risk score >80. UEBA risk scores aggregate multiple weak signals into a composite risk indicator. Integrate risk scores into alert triage—a medium-severity alert from a high-risk user should auto-escalate.

criteria.text: Identity anomaly detection is enabled (impossible travel, new device, unusual hours)
criteria.recommendation: Enable identity anomaly detection models: impossible travel (logon from two geographically impossible locations within a short window), new device authentication, logon outside established hours, unusual access volume. These are among the most reliable indicators of account compromise.

criteria.text: Privilege escalation detection is enabled and generating actionable alerts
criteria.recommendation: Enable UEBA privilege escalation detection models. The 13000-series UEBA includes native detection for: unusual privilege use, new admin group membership, service account used interactively, and role expansion outside change windows. Configure these to trigger high-priority alerts surfaced in SIEM alert triage.

criteria.text: UEBA alerts are surfaced within Log360 SIEM alert triage workflow
criteria.recommendation: Verify that UEBA anomaly alerts are integrated with the main Log360 alert console. In the 13000 series, UEBA is natively integrated—anomaly alerts should appear in the main Incidents dashboard with MITRE ATT&CK context. If UEBA alerts are isolated in a separate module, enable the native integration.

criteria.text: Entity analytics (servers, applications) are enabled in addition to user analytics
criteria.recommendation: Enable entity-level behavior analytics for servers and applications in addition to user analytics. Server anomalies (unusual outbound connections, process execution patterns, login patterns) are critical for detecting malware activity, C2 communication, and data staging. Navigate to UEBA > Entity Analytics.

criteria.text: CrowdStrike Falcon EDR integration is configured for endpoint telemetry and response
criteria.recommendation: Configure CrowdStrike Falcon integration via API to: pull endpoint telemetry (process activity, network connections, file writes) into Log360, enable automated endpoint isolation via SOAR, and enrich Log360 alerts with endpoint context. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > EDR > CrowdStrike.

criteria.text: BitDefender or other EDR integration is configured
criteria.recommendation: Configure your EDR/AV solution integration (BitDefender GravityZone, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, SentinelOne, etc.) to forward detection events to Log360. EDR telemetry significantly improves detection fidelity by adding process and memory-level visibility unavailable from Windows Event Logs alone.

criteria.text: Centralized integration management is configured for all connected products
criteria.recommendation: Use Log360's Centralized Integration Management console to manage all product integrations from a single interface. Verify all integration health statuses are green. Configure alerts for integration health failures to detect when an integration silently stops working. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > Integration Health.

criteria.text: NetFlow Analyzer integration is configured for network traffic visibility
criteria.recommendation: Configure the native NetFlow Analyzer integration to add network flow data to Log360 SIEM. NetFlow data enables: detection of data exfiltration by volume, C2 beaconing patterns, lateral movement by connection patterns, and DNS tunneling. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > ManageEngine > NetFlow Analyzer.

criteria.text: Firewall Analyzer integration is configured for firewall log correlation
criteria.recommendation: Configure the native Firewall Analyzer integration to forward firewall audit logs to Log360. This enables cross-product correlation: a firewall policy change correlated with failed VPN authentication attempts points to an attacker attempting to disable perimeter controls. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > ManageEngine > Firewall Analyzer.

criteria.text: OpManager network monitoring integration is configured for operational context
criteria.recommendation: Configure OpManager audit log forwarding to Log360. Network device operational events (interface down, device unreachable, configuration change) provide context that distinguishes security incidents from operational issues. Correlate OpManager events with security events for better incident classification.

criteria.text: ServiceDesk Plus / ITSM integration is configured for automated incident ticket creation
criteria.recommendation: Configure bidirectional ITSM integration: Log360 auto-creates incident tickets for high-severity alerts, and resolved tickets automatically close Log360 incidents. This creates an accountable incident management process and provides audit evidence of response activities. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > ITSM.

criteria.text: Incident workflow is defined with severity levels, assignment rules, and SLAs
criteria.recommendation: Define incident management workflows: severity classification (P1-P4), auto-assignment rules (route cloud incidents to cloud team), SLA targets (P1: 1hr, P2: 4hr, P3: 24hr, P4: 72hr), and escalation procedures. Navigate to Incidents > Workflow Configuration.

criteria.text: Incident timeline and evidence documentation is used for all security incidents
criteria.recommendation: Train analysts to use Log360's incident timeline feature to document: timeline of events, affected systems, actions taken, evidence collected, and post-incident findings. This documentation is required for regulatory reporting (GDPR breach notification, PCI incident response documentation).

criteria.text: Expanded compliance packs are deployed for applicable frameworks
criteria.recommendation: Deploy all applicable compliance packs from the Log360 marketplace: PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOX, GDPR, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, FISMA, CCPA, and industry-specific frameworks. Compliance packs provide pre-built dashboards, reports, and alert rules mapped to control requirements. Navigate to Compliance > Marketplace.

criteria.text: Compliance dashboard shows current compliance posture for all frameworks
criteria.recommendation: Configure the compliance dashboard to show real-time compliance posture for all applicable frameworks. Schedule automated compliance reports for delivery to GRC team, auditors, and CISO. Use the compliance gap analysis to prioritize remediation efforts.

criteria.text: HTTPS / TLS 1.2+ is enforced with valid SSL certificate and weak ciphers disabled
criteria.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS-only access to Log360 console. Configure TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites (AES-256, SHA-256). Disable: TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, SSLv3, RC4, DES, 3DES, NULL ciphers, EXPORT ciphers. Install a certificate from your enterprise CA. Configure HTTP to HTTPS redirect.

criteria.text: Two-factor authentication is mandatory for all Log360 administrator accounts
criteria.recommendation: Enforce 2FA for all Log360 accounts, especially administrators. Log360 provides visibility into your entire security posture—a compromised admin account would allow attackers to disable alerts, delete evidence, and view your detection capabilities. Enable under Admin > Security > MFA.

criteria.text: Product activity audit log is enabled and protected from modification
criteria.recommendation: Enable and protect the Log360 product activity audit log. This log must capture: who logged in, what reports/queries they ran, what configuration changes were made, and what data was exported. This is critical for detecting insider misuse of the SIEM and maintaining audit chain of custody.

criteria.text: Log collection failure alerting is configured for critical log sources
criteria.recommendation: Configure alerts for log collection failures on critical sources (DCs, firewalls, WAF, critical servers). A silent log source failure creates a security blind spot—attackers know to target systems that aren't being monitored. Set threshold: alert if source silent >15 minutes for critical, >60 minutes for standard sources.
